fitness-sparse.content:
# WELCOME EMAIL SEQUENCE
## Marcus Reed — Reed Performance

---

## EMAIL 1: WELCOME + DELIVERY
**Send:** Immediately after opt-in  
**Purpose:** Deliver lead magnet, set expectations, build immediate trust

**Subject Line:** Your 15-minute workout is here (+ what's coming next)
**Alt Subject:** Here's your executive workout guide

**Preview Text:** Download link inside + 6 more emails that could change everything

**Body Copy:**

You just downloaded The Executive's 15-Minute Workout — here's your link:

[DOWNLOAD: The Executive's 15-Minute Workout]

This isn't some generic bodyweight routine. It's specifically designed for executives who need maximum results in minimum time, using equipment you already have in your home office.

Try it once this week. You'll feel the difference.

Over the next [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here], I'm sending you 6 more emails that walk through exactly how executives like Dan M. (SaaS CEO) lost 42 pounds while traveling 2 weeks per month, and Rick P. (private equity partner) dropped his A1C from prediabetic to optimal.

These aren't feel-good stories. They're case studies in what works when you have a 60-hour work week and can't live at the gym.

At this point, the obvious question is, how can you apply The Performance Stack and get a custom baseline, your first 15-20 pound loss plus strength baseline, and complete transformation today?

Well, you basically have two options...

You can take what I've shared with you and try to implement it all yourself, with nobody to review your progress, adjust your protocols when business demands change, and provide immediate support when you hit obstacles... leaving you to figure out all the details yourself through trial and error...

Or you can apply for The Executive Athlete Program and get the proven system that gets custom baselines, first-phase transformations, and complete body recomposition...

I'm closing enrollment for The Executive Athlete Program tonight at 11:59pm EST.

Here's what you get: 6 months of The Performance Stack, custom protocols for your schedule, weekly video reviews, biweekly 1-on-1 calls, private Slack access, and complete travel protocols. $12,000 total or 6 monthly payments of $2,100.

If you've read every email and you're STILL thinking about it — that hesitation is exactly what's keeping you stuck as The Peloton Paperweight CEO.

In 6 hours, this offer closes. You can either be the same person tomorrow, or you can be someone who took action to become The Executive Athlete.

Now is the time to decide!

--- Apply Before 11:59pm ---
[reedperformance.co/apply]

Marcus

**CTA:** Apply for The Executive Athlete Program
**P.S.:** This opportunity closes after 11:59pm tonight.

---

## Email 12: Final 2-Hour Deadline
**Send: Day 10 (6 hours after Email 11)**

**Subject Line:** 2 hours left
**Alt Subject:** Last chance

**Preview Text:** The door is closing...

**Body Copy:**

Hey %FIRSTNAME%,

REAL QUICK:

In less than 2 hours, my client roster is closing for the foreseeable future.

So, I want you to take a second to think about...

What are the next 6 months going to look like for you?

Struggling with no time to train, trainers who don't understand your schedule, doing generic fitness programs, getting frustrated with starting and stopping routines and feeling embarrassed when you catch your reflection?

Or...

Following a proven method to get a custom baseline, your first 15-20 pound loss plus strength baseline, and complete transformation and ultimately get in the best shape of your life in the next 6 months and become The Executive Athlete.

Those are your options. Which will you choose?

You've seen the framework. You've seen the results. You know the price.

The only question is: are you ready to become The Executive Athlete?

This Offer Closes at 11:59 PM EST TONIGHT (less than 2 hours from now...)

--- Apply Now ---
[reedperformance.co/apply]

Either way, I respect your decision. But if you're in — now's the time.

Marcus

**CTA:** Apply for The Executive Athlete Program
**P.S.:** If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days while following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

fitness-sparse.content:
# CONTENT ANGLE LIBRARY
## Marcus Reed — Reed Performance

---

## SECTION 1: MIDDLE OF FUNNEL (MOF) — 93+ ANGLES

### A. IDENTITY & MINDSET (13 ANGLES)

| # | Hook (first line) | Target Belief (1-6) | Evidence Type | Format | Brief Outline (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Your clients are a mirror of your fitness standards — if they're making excuses and skipping workouts, look in the mirror. | 6 | Personal Story | Video/Post | Share how when you were making excuses about your own health while running the gym, you attracted clients who did the same. The moment you got serious about The Performance Stack, you started attracting executives who were serious about results. |
| 2 | If you hesitate to invest in your own health, your clients will hesitate to invest in theirs. | 4 | Universal Analogy | Post | Compare how executives who won't spend on their own transformation signal to prospects that the investment isn't worth it. Your confidence in the value comes from experiencing the value yourself. |
| 3 | How you spend your money reveals who you really are — not who you think you are. | 4 | Self-Assessment | Reel | Break down the spending patterns of The Peloton Paperweight CEO vs The Executive Athlete. One spends on equipment that collects dust, the other invests in systems that deliver results. |
| 4 | You become who you surround yourself with — that's why most executives stay out of shape. | 1 | Authority Reference | Post | Reference Jim Collins' research on peer influence and apply it to executive fitness. Most executives surround themselves with other out-of-shape executives who normalize declining health. |
| 5 | Confused executives don't buy — and confused messaging is why your fitness goals fail. | 1 | Case Study | Video | Show how unclear goals like "get in better shape" lead to random actions and no results. Clear goals like "lose 30 lbs in 6 months" create clear actions and measurable progress. |
| 6 | Millionaire executives track their body metrics daily — broke executives track Netflix episodes. | 3 | Cross-Domain | Reel | Compare the daily habits of successful vs unsuccessful executives. Winners measure what matters: weight, energy levels, workout completion. Losers measure entertainment consumption. |
| 7 | Your relationship with investing in health dictates your body transformation results. | 4 | Personal Story | Post | Tell the story of executives who see health spending as an expense vs those who see it as an investment. The mindset determines the outcome before the program even starts. |
| 8 | How you spend your time reveals your real priorities — not what you say they are. | 3 | Universal Story | Video | Two executives both say health is important. One spends 2 hours on social media daily but "has no time" for 30-minute workouts. The other finds time because it's actually a priority. |
| 9 | Fast health decisions separate top performers from everyone else. | 6 | Internal Study | Post | Reference how executives who decide quickly on The Performance Stack get results faster than those who "think about it" for months. Decision speed correlates with implementation speed. |
| 10 | You can't hit a fitness target you can't clearly see. | 2 | Authority Reference | Reel | Use goal-setting research to show why vague goals like "get healthy" fail while specific goals like "squat 2x bodyweight" succeed. Vision clarity drives action clarity. |
| 11 | Becoming The Executive Athlete requires identity-level change, not just behavior change. | 1 | Client Case Study | Video | Show how Dan M. had to stop seeing himself as "too busy for fitness" and start seeing himself as someone who makes fitness work with any schedule. Identity shift preceded the 42-lb transformation. |
| 12 | Comfort is the enemy of progress — that's why your current routine isn't working. | 2 | Universal Analogy | Post | Compare business growth to physical growth. Both require stepping outside comfort zones. Executives who stay comfortable with declining health never transform. |
| 13 | The speed of your health decisions separates you from executives who stay stuck for years. | 6 | Cross-Domain | Reel | Show how successful executives make business decisions quickly but health decisions slowly. The same decisiveness that built their business needs to be applied to their body. |

### B. THE REAL PROBLEM (11 ANGLES)

| # | Hook (first line) | Target Belief (1-6) | Evidence Type | Format | Brief Outline (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | You think your problem is lack of willpower — it's actually lack of a system that fits your real life. | 2 | Problem Reframe | Video | Break down why executives blame themselves for failing at generic programs when the real issue is trying to force square pegs into round holes. The Performance Stack solves the system problem, not the willpower problem. |
| 15 | The real structure of your fitness problem: you're using consumer solutions for enterprise-level demands. | 1 | Universal Analogy | Post | Explain how $5M businesses need enterprise software, not QuickBooks. Executive fitness needs executive-level systems, not consumer-grade gym memberships. |
| 16 | Why every trainer you've hired failed: they designed programs for their ideal client, not your actual life. | 5 | Client Case Study | Reel | Show how previous trainers gave Dan M. programs assuming he had predictable schedules and local gym access. The Performance Stack was designed around his actual constraints: travel, long hours, unpredictable demands. |
| 17 | Before anything works, this must be true: your system must sync with your actual schedule, not fight against it. | 1 | Authority Reference | Video | Reference systems thinking principles and apply them to executive fitness. Success requires alignment between system design and user constraints, not forcing mismatched solutions. |
| 18 | Here's where you are today: 42 lbs heavier, catching your reflection and immediately looking away. | 2 | Current State | Post | Paint the brutal reality of The Peloton Paperweight CEO using specific details from the client's unwanted feelings. Create urgency through honest assessment of current trajectory. |
| 19 | Every month of delay = another month closer to the heart attack his father had at 58. Plus ~$500/month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers. | 3 | Cost Analysis | Reel | Use the coach-provided monthly action cost verbatim to show the real price of procrastination. Make inaction more expensive than action. |
| 20 | One unaddressed health problem creates five more: low energy leads to poor decisions, poor decisions lead to business stress, stress leads to worse health. | 2 | Consequence Cascade | Video | Map out how declining physical health creates a downward spiral affecting every area of an executive's life. Show how The Performance Stack breaks the cycle. |
| 21 | You're chasing the wrong goal — you want to "get in shape" when you need to "build a system that keeps you in shape." | 1 | Goal Correction | Post | Reframe from outcome goals to system goals. Getting in shape is temporary; building The Performance Stack is permanent. |
| 22 | Ask yourself: How long have you been saying "I'll start Monday" and what has that cost you? | 2 | Self-Assessment | Reel | Force honest self-reflection about procrastination patterns. Connect the dots between delayed action and current frustration with their body. |
| 23 | You've been stuck for 3-5 years — that's not a discipline problem, that's a system problem. | 2 | Problem Duration | Video | Reframe long-term struggle as evidence that willpower isn't the issue. If discipline was the problem, they would have solved it by now given their business success. |
| 24 | The real price of "I'll start after this quarter ends": another quarter of declining energy, expanding waistline, and missed opportunities. | 3 | Procrastination Cost | Post | Show how "perfect timing" never comes and each delay compounds the problem. The best time was yesterday, the second best time is now. |

### C. THE SOLUTION (17 ANGLES)

| # | Hook (first line) | Target Belief (1-6) | Evidence Type | Format | Brief Outline (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 25 | Old way: Generic programs that assume 9-to-5 schedules. New way: The Performance Stack syncs to your 60-hour work week. | 1 | Method Comparison | Video | Contrast traditional fitness approaches with The Performance Stack's executive-specific design. Show why location-independent, schedule-flexible systems outperform gym-dependent programs. |
| 26 | How I discovered this approach: A COVID client asked for Zoom training and opened my eyes to the power of location-independent systems. | 1 | Discovery Story | Post | Tell the turning point story of realizing that remote systems could be more effective than in-person training for busy executives. |
| 27 | The biggest lie in executive fitness: "You just need more discipline and willpower." | 2 | Myth Debunk | Reel | Expose how the fitness industry blames individuals for system failures. Discipline isn't the problem when you're making critical business decisions all day. |
| 28 | The trap that keeps executives stuck: trying to force consumer fitness solutions into enterprise-level lives. | 1 | Trap ID | Video | Identify the mismatch between available solutions and executive constraints. Show how The Performance Stack was built specifically for this gap. |
| 29 | What the fitness industry doesn't want you to know: location-dependent training is designed for their convenience, not your results. | 5 | Industry Secret | Post | Reveal how traditional personal training models prioritize trainer scheduling over client results. Remote systems prioritize client outcomes over trainer convenience. |
| 30 | What most people get wrong about executive fitness: they think it's about finding time when it's about designing systems. | 1 | Misunderstanding | Reel | Correct the common belief that busy executives need to find more time. The solution is building systems that work within existing time constraints. |
| 31 | Why the popular "just go to the gym" approach is fundamentally broken for executives who travel 40% of the time. | 5 | Method Rejection | Video | Break down why location-dependent solutions fail for executives with unpredictable schedules and frequent travel. |
| 32 | The 30,000-foot view of The Performance Stack: 3 layers that sync training, nutrition, and recovery to your work rhythm. | 1 | Process Overview | Post | Explain the high-level concept of how the system works without getting into tactical details. Focus on the integration aspect. |
| 33 | What the first 3 weeks look like: clients drop 5-8 lbs and feel noticeably stronger before month 1 ends. | 1 | Process Walkthrough | Reel | Walk through the early experience using the coach-provided timeframe. Build confidence that results come quickly with the right system. |
| 34 | Why each phase matters: Skip Assessment and you're guessing. Skip Rebuild and you plateau. Skip Scale and you backslide. | 1 | Phase Importance | Video | Explain why the 3-phase system requires all phases for lasting transformation. Show the consequences of trying to shortcut the process. |
| 35 | The science behind why this works: systems thinking applied to human performance optimization. | 1 | Theory Foundation | Post | Reference systems theory and explain how The Performance Stack applies proven principles to executive fitness challenges. |
| 36 | Live example: How Rick P. maintained his nutrition protocol during a 3-city business trip. | 1 | Live Demo | Reel | Show the system in action with a real client example. Demonstrate how the travel protocols work in practice. |
| 37 | The non-negotiable rules for getting results: consistency over intensity, systems over motivation, progress over perfection. | 1 | Success Principles | Video | Lay out the fundamental principles that make The Performance Stack effective. Show why these rules matter more than specific tactics. |
| 38 | How to know it's working: energy stays consistent past 3pm, clothes fit better, strength numbers go up weekly. | 1 | Progress Measurement | Post | Give specific leading indicators that show the system is working before major weight loss occurs. Build confidence in the process. |
| 39 | What life looks like after 6 months: squatting 2x bodyweight, traveling 2 weeks per month and staying on track, taking your shirt off without sucking in. | 1 | Outcome Vision | Reel | Paint the picture of The Executive Athlete transformation using specific client results. Make the outcome tangible and desirable. |
| 40 | Timeline comparison: 3+ years of failed attempts vs 6 months of systematic transformation. | 1 | Timeline Compare | Video | Contrast the time wasted on ineffective approaches with the speed of results from The Performance Stack. |
| 41 | Quick wins that build confidence: first week energy improvement, second week strength gains, third week visible changes. | 1 | Quick Wins | Post | Show how early wins create momentum for bigger transformations. Reference the coach-provided 3-week timeframe for credibility. |

### D. WHY YOU (16 ANGLES)

| # | Hook (first line) | Target Belief (1-6) | Evidence Type | Format | Brief Outline (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 42 | I help busy executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office. | 6 | Mission Level 1 | Post | State the basic value proposition clearly and directly. Focus on the specific niche and unique constraint (home office). |
| 43 | The deeper transformation: I help executives prove to themselves that they can optimize their body the same way they optimized their business. | 6 | Mission Level 2 | Video | Connect physical transformation to business identity. Show how mastering health reinforces their identity as someone who masters challenges. |
| 44 | The ripple effect: When executives model peak performance in health, they elevate their entire organization's standards. | 6 | Mission Level 3 | Reel | Explain how executive health transformation impacts company culture, family dynamics, and industry leadership standards. |
| 45 | My mission: Help executives who've given up on themselves prove their own critics wrong. | 6 | Mission Statement | Post | State the emotional core of the work. Connect to the deeper motivation beyond just physical transformation. |
| 46 | My core belief: High performance in business and fitness aren't mutually exclusive — they're complementary. | 6 | Thesis | Video | Present the fundamental philosophy that drives The Performance Stack. Challenge the false choice between business success and health. |
| 47 | 11 years of coaching, 340 clients, and the journey from brick-and-mortar burnout to online transformation specialist. | 6 | Experience Doc | Reel | Summarize the credibility markers and transformation story that built this expertise. Use only verified facts from the client data. |
| 48 | What I stand for: Systems over willpower, data over guessing, results over excuses. What I stand against: Generic programs, location dependency, one-size-fits-all solutions. | 6 | Values/Positions | Post | Clearly state the philosophical positions that differentiate this approach from competitors. |
| 49 | Dan M., SaaS CEO: Lost 42 lbs, squats 2x bodyweight, travels 2 weeks/month and stays on track. "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like." | 6 | Client Spotlight | Video | Showcase the ideal client transformation with specific metrics and authentic testimonial. |
| 50 | The proof: 52 out of 60 clients achieved their target results. First measurable wins show up within the first 3 weeks. | 6 | Results Proof | Reel | Present the success rate and timeline data using verified numbers from the client input. |
| 51 | "Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like." — Dan M., SaaS CEO who lost 42 lbs while traveling constantly. | 6 | Testimonial | Post | Lead with the authentic client voice that captures the unique value proposition. |
| 52 | Best case study: Rick P. went from prediabetic A1C of 6.2 to optimal 5.4 while losing 28 lbs in 6 months. "My doctor couldn't believe the numbers." | 6 | Case Study Best | Video | Tell the complete transformation story with specific health metrics and medical validation. |
| 53 | Worst case: Executives who don't implement stay stuck in the cycle of buying equipment they don't use and hiring trainers they cancel on. | 6 | Case Study Worst | Reel | Show the cost of inaction by describing the continued frustration of The Peloton Paperweight CEO. |
| 54 | My background: From the fat kid nobody believed in to helping executives prove their critics wrong. | 6 | Personal Background | Post | Share the origin story that creates relatability and emotional connection with the mission. |
| 55 | I don't work with executives who aren't willing to take responsibility for their results or follow proven systems. | 6 | Client Rejection | Video | Set boundaries around ideal client characteristics. Show that this is for serious people only. |

| 104 | Investment breakdown: $12,000 for 6 months vs. $500/month wasted on unused memberships and canceled trainers. | Video | Show the math of effective vs wasteful spending. Compare the total cost to what they're already spending on ineffective solutions. |
| 105 | Who this is for: Male executives age 40-55, company revenue $500K-$5M, haven't prioritized health in years. | Post | Define the ideal client clearly. Help prospects self-select in or out based on specific criteria. |
| 106 | The Executive Athlete Program vs. local trainers vs. generic online programs. | Reel | Create a comparison chart showing why The Performance Stack outperforms alternatives for busy executives. |
| 107 | Frequently asked questions: "Can this really work with my travel schedule?" | Video | Address the top objections with specific examples and proof. Show how the system adapts to executive constraints. |
| 108 | Application process: 5 minutes to apply, 24 hours to hear back, immediate start for qualified candidates. | Post | Explain the next steps clearly. Make the application process feel simple and the response time fast. |

## SECTION 3: YOUTUBE LONG-FORM SCRIPTS (10 TITLES + OUTLINES)

### 1. **Title:** "Why Every Personal Trainer You've Hired Has Failed (And What Actually Works for Busy Executives)"
- **Hook:** "In this video, I'm going to show you why every trainer you've hired has failed to get you lasting results, and what actually works for executives who work 60-hour weeks, so you can finally get in the best shape of your life without fighting your schedule."

- **Target Belief:** #2 (Real Problem)

  3. Proof: Rick P. case study - A1C from 6.2 to 5.4 using systematic approach
- **CTA:** Download The Executive's 15-Minute Workout at reedperformance.co/15-minute-workout

### 3. **Title:** "Why Successful Executives Stay Out of Shape (And How to Break the Cycle)"

- **Target Belief:** #3 (Time Priority)

### 5. **Title:** "Why Online Coaching Beats In-Person Training for Busy Executives"

### 6. **Title:** "Why Smart Executives Try to Figure Out Fitness Alone (And Why It Never Works)"

  3. Proof: Walk through the 3 phases and show real client results
- **CTA:** Download The Executive's 15-Minute Workout at reedperformance.co/15-minute-workout

### 8. **Title:** "From Peloton Paperweight CEO to Executive Athlete in 6 Months"
- **Hook:** "In this video, I'm going to show you how to go from Peloton Paperweight CEO to Executive Athlete in 6 months using a proven system."
- **Target Belief:** #1 (Core Belief)

- **Target Belief:** #3 (Time Priority)

fitness-sparse.content:
# GHL CHAT/SMS AUTOMATION SEQUENCE
## Marcus Reed — Reed Performance

---

#### STEP 1: Initial Response & Qualification Setup

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact sends any message to the business number or responds to any marketing campaign

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds with any message: → Continue to Step 2
> - IF no response after 2 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: lead-engaged] | [+Add: qualification-started]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualification]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey there! Marcus here from Reed Performance.

I help busy executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

Quick question — are you currently running your own company and making $300K+ annually?

Just want to make sure we're a good fit before we dive deeper.

---

#### STEP 2: Revenue Qualification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies YES to Step 1 question about running company and income level

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact replies YES: → Continue to Step 3
> - IF contact replies NO: → Go to Step 9 (Disqualification sequence)
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: revenue-qualified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualification]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. Here's what I need to know:

How long have you been putting off getting in serious shape? 

And what's the biggest challenge you're facing right now — no time to train, trainers who don't get your schedule, or programs that fall apart when business gets demanding?

---

#### STEP 3: Problem Identification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact replies to Step 2 with their timeline and main challenge

**Time Delay:** Wait 3 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact mentions time/schedule issues: → Continue to Step 4
> - IF contact mentions failed programs: → Continue to Step 4  
> - IF contact gives vague answer: → Go to Step 10 (Clarification request)
> - IF no response after 6 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: problem-identified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualification]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got it. That's exactly what I hear from most executives.

Here's the thing — you don't need more willpower. You need a system that actually fits the reality of a 60-hour work week.

One more question: Are you ready to invest $12,000 over 6 months to solve this problem for good, or are you still in the "looking around" phase?

I only work with executives who are serious about getting results.

---

#### STEP 4: Investment Qualification

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact indicates readiness to invest or asks about the program

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact says ready to invest: → Continue to Step 5
> - IF contact asks about payment plans: → Continue to Step 5
> - IF contact balks at price: → Go to Step 11 (Price objection sequence)
> - IF no response after 8 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: investment-qualified]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualified-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Excellent. 

Last question: On a scale of 1-10, how committed are you to following a proven system for 6 months straight — even when you're traveling, dealing with business crises, or working 16-hour days?

I need to know you're not going to be another executive who starts strong then disappears when Q4 gets crazy.

---

#### STEP 5: Commitment Assessment & Program Introduction

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact rates their commitment level (looking for 7+ out of 10)

**Time Delay:** Wait 1 minute

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact rates 7-10: → Continue to Step 6
> - IF contact rates 5-6: → Go to Step 12 (Commitment concern sequence)
> - IF contact rates 1-4: → Go to Step 9 (Disqualification sequence)
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: commitment-qualified] | [+Add: hot-prospect]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: qualified-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. You sound like exactly the type of executive I work with.

Here's what I've got for you:

The Executive Athlete Program — a 6-month system that helps executives lose 30+ pounds and double their strength without missing business meetings.

It's built around The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your actual schedule, not some fantasy 9-to-5 life.

Dan M. (SaaS CEO) lost 42 pounds while traveling 2 weeks per month. Rick P. (private equity) dropped his A1C from prediabetic to optimal.

Want to see exactly how it works?

---

#### STEP 6: Program Details & Application

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expresses interest in learning more about the program

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact says YES: → Continue to Step 7
> - IF contact asks specific questions: → Continue to Step 7
> - IF contact seems hesitant: → Go to Step 13 (Hesitation handling)
> - IF no response after 6 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: program-interested]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-ready]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Here's how it works:

**Phase 1 (Assessment):** 3 video calls + bloodwork review = custom baseline
**Phase 2 (Rebuild):** 8 weeks in-home strength + nutrition = first 15-20 lb loss  
**Phase 3 (Scale):** 12 weeks progressive loading + travel protocols = complete transformation

You get: Custom programs, weekly video reviews, biweekly 1-on-1 calls, private Slack access, hotel/travel protocols.

Investment: $12,000 for 6 months (or 6 payments of $2,100)

Guarantee: If you don't lose 20 lbs in the first 90 days following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

Ready to apply? Takes 5 minutes: reedperformance.co/apply

---

#### STEP 7: Application Submission & Next Steps

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact indicates they want to apply or asks about next steps

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact submits application: → Go to Step 14 (Application received sequence)
> - IF contact asks more questions: → Go to Step 15 (FAQ sequence)
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-sent]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-pending]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Perfect. Here's what happens next:

1. Fill out the application: reedperformance.co/apply (takes 5 minutes)
2. I'll review it within 24 hours  
3. If you're a fit, we'll schedule a brief call to get you started
4. If not, I'll let you know why and point you toward better resources

Fair warning: I only take 12 clients at a time. If you're serious about this, don't wait.

Every month you delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58. Plus you're wasting $500/month on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers.

Apply now: reedperformance.co/apply

---

#### STEP 8: Follow-up Sequence (No Response)

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hasn't responded to previous message within specified timeframe

**Time Delay:** Wait 4 hours from last message

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Return to appropriate step based on context
> - IF no response after 24 hours: → Go to Step 16 (Final follow-up)
> - IF contact says "not interested": → Go to Step 17 (Graceful exit)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: follow-up-needed]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Hey, I know you're busy running a company.

Just wanted to make sure you saw my last message about The Executive Athlete Program.

If the timing isn't right, no worries. But if you're serious about getting in the best shape of your life without sacrificing your business, this is exactly what you need.

Quick yes or no — should I hold your spot while you think it over?

---

#### STEP 9: Disqualification Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact doesn't meet revenue requirements or commitment level

**Time Delay:** Wait 1 minute

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact asks for alternatives: → Go to Step 18 (Alternative resources)
> - IF no response: → End sequence after 24 hours

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: disqualified] | [-Remove: all-qualification-tags]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: disqualified]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I appreciate your honesty. 

The Executive Athlete Program is specifically designed for executives making $300K+ who can invest $12,000 in their transformation.

If your situation changes, feel free to reach out. In the meantime, here's a free resource that might help: reedperformance.co/15-minute-workout

Best of luck with your health journey.

---

#### STEP 10: Clarification Request

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact gives vague or unclear answer to problem identification question

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact provides clearer answer: → Return to Step 3
> - IF contact remains vague: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: needs-clarification]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Let me be more specific.

Most executives I work with struggle with one of these:

1. No time to train with their 60-hour work weeks
2. Trainers who don't understand business travel demands  
3. Programs that fall apart during quarterly crunches

Which one hits closest to home for you?

---

#### STEP 11: Price Objection Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact expresses concern about the $12,000 investment

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact asks about payment plans: → Continue to Step 4
> - IF contact says it's too expensive: → Continue with objection handling
> - IF no response after 6 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: price-objection]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I get it. $12,000 feels like a lot.

But here's what you're already spending: $500/month on unused memberships and canceled trainers. That's [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]per year with zero results.

Plus the cost of declining performance. How much is one bad decision worth when you're running on fumes at 3pm?

This pays for itself in improved productivity alone. And we offer 6 monthly payments of $2,100.

The real question: Can you afford NOT to fix this?

---

#### STEP 12: Commitment Concern Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact rates commitment level as 5-6 out of 10

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact explains and shows higher commitment: → Continue to Step 6
> - IF contact remains uncertain: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: commitment-concern]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I appreciate the honesty.

Here's the thing — I've worked with 340 clients over 11 years. The ones who succeed aren't the ones with perfect schedules. They're the ones who commit to the system even when business gets crazy.

Dan M. closed a $2M deal during month 3 of his transformation. Didn't miss a single workout.

If you're not ready to prioritize this, that's fine. But don't waste both our time if you're going to quit when Q4 gets busy.

What's it going to be?

---

#### STEP 13: Hesitation Handling

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact seems interested but hesitant about moving forward

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact asks specific questions: → Go to Step 15 (FAQ sequence)
> - IF contact expresses interest: → Return to Step 7
> - IF no response after 4 hours: → Go to Step 8 (Follow-up sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: hesitant-prospect]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Let me guess what you're thinking:

"I don't have time for another commitment."
"Online coaching can't replace in-person."
"I've tried this before and it didn't stick."

Right?

Here's the truth: You've never tried a system built specifically for executives who work 60-hour weeks and travel constantly.

Every program you've tried was designed for people with predictable schedules and local gym access.

This is different. This is built for your actual life.

What's your biggest concern?

---

#### STEP 14: Application Received Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact submits application through the provided link

**Time Delay:** Wait 30 minutes

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF application is approved: → Go to Step 19 (Approval sequence)
> - IF application needs clarification: → Go to Step 20 (Clarification needed)
> - IF application is declined: → Go to Step 21 (Decline sequence)

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: application-submitted] | [+Add: review-pending]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: application-review]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Got your application. Thanks for taking the time to fill it out properly.

I'm reviewing it now and will get back to you within 24 hours with next steps.

If you're approved, we'll schedule a brief call to get you started immediately. If not, I'll explain why and point you toward better resources.

Either way, you'll hear from me soon.

Talk soon.

---

#### STEP 15: FAQ Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact asks specific questions about the program

**Time Delay:** Wait 1 minute

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF question is about time commitment: → Address time objection
> - IF question is about online vs in-person: → Address method objection  
> - IF question is about results: → Provide case studies
> - IF satisfied with answer: → Return to Step 7

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: has-questions]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Great question. Let me address that:

**Time commitment:** 4 hours per week total. That's less time than you spend in traffic.

**Online vs in-person:** The system travels with you. Dan trains in Austin, New York, and Tokyo. Same results everywhere.

**Guaranteed results:** If you don't lose 20 lbs in 90 days following the protocol, I work with you free until you do.

**Travel concerns:** We have specific hotel gym protocols. You'll never miss a workout due to location.

What else do you need to know?

---

#### STEP 16: Final Follow-up

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact hasn't responded after multiple follow-up attempts

**Time Delay:** Wait 24 hours from last follow-up

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds: → Return to appropriate step
> - IF no response: → End sequence and tag as "cold lead"

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: final-attempt] | [+Add: cold-lead]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: cold-lead]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

Last message from me.

I get it — you're busy. Running a company is demanding.

But here's what I know: Every month you wait is another month closer to the health crisis you're trying to avoid.

If you change your mind, you know where to find me.

Otherwise, I wish you the best.

---

#### STEP 17: Graceful Exit

**Trigger Condition:**
> Contact explicitly says they're not interested

**Time Delay:** None (immediate response)

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: not-interested] | [-Remove: all-active-tags]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: not-interested]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

No problem at all. I appreciate you being direct.

If your situation changes, feel free to reach out.

Here's a free resource in the meantime: reedperformance.co/15-minute-workout

Best of luck with everything.

---

#### STEP 18: Alternative Resources

**Trigger Condition:**
> Disqualified contact asks for alternatives or other help

**Time Delay:** Wait 1 minute

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - End sequence after this message

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: alternative-provided]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Keep current stage]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

I respect that you're looking for solutions even if we're not a fit right now.

Here's what I'd recommend:

1. Start with this free guide: reedperformance.co/15-minute-workout
2. Focus on consistency over intensity
3. When you're ready to invest in a proven system, reach out

Good luck with your health journey.

---

#### STEP 19: Application Approval Sequence

**Trigger Condition:**
> Application is reviewed and approved

**Time Delay:** Wait 2 hours

**Condition Check (If/Then):**
> - IF contact responds positively: → Schedule call
> - IF contact has questions: → Answer and proceed
> - IF no response after 12 hours: → Follow up once

**Tags to Apply:** [+Add: approved-client] | [+Add: ready-to-start]
**Pipeline Stage:** [Move to: approved-client]

**Message Content (copy-paste ready):**

**Weekend Response:** Delayed until next business day

fitness-sparse.content:
# FACEBOOK CONTENT PLAYBOOK
## Marcus Reed — Reed Performance

---

## POST 1 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Executives who engage with fitness content  

You're training in hotel gyms wrong.

Most executives try to replicate their home routine when they travel. Wrong move.

Hotel gyms have limited equipment and weird hours. Fighting that reality is why you skip workouts on business trips.

Here's what actually works:

**The 15-Minute Hotel Room Protocol:**
- Bodyweight squats: 3 sets of 15
- Push-ups (modify on knees if needed): 3 sets of 10
- Plank hold: 3 sets of 30 seconds
- Mountain climbers: 3 sets of 20

No equipment needed. Works in a 6x6 space. Takes 15 minutes.

Dan travels 2 weeks per month and hasn't missed a workout in 6 months using this approach.

The goal isn't to get the perfect workout. The goal is to stay consistent regardless of location.

What's your biggest challenge when trying to stay on track while traveling?

---

## POST 2 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Business owners, entrepreneurs  

2021 was my rock bottom.

Training 12 hours a day at my gym. Barely breaking even after lease and payroll.

I was burnt out, gaining weight, and resenting the clients I was supposed to be helping.

The irony wasn't lost on me — I was a fitness professional who couldn't maintain his own fitness.

Then COVID hit. A client asked if I could train him over Zoom.

I said yes, mainly because I needed the money.

That single session changed everything.

I realized I could help just as many people without the overhead, scheduling conflicts, and location constraints.

I closed the gym in 2022 and went fully online with customized home programs.

Now I help executives get in the best shape of their lives without leaving their home office.

Sometimes your biggest failure points you toward your biggest breakthrough.

The system that was killing me became the foundation for something better.

---

## POST 3 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Executives who've tried multiple fitness programs  

You think your problem is willpower.

It's actually a system problem.

Here's why:

You make 35,000 decisions per day as an executive. Each decision depletes your mental energy.

By 6pm, when you're supposed to hit the gym, you have zero willpower left.

That's not a character flaw. That's biology.

You wouldn't run your company by hoping employees "stay motivated" every day.

You build systems, processes, and structures that produce results regardless of individual energy levels.

Your fitness should work the same way.

The Performance Stack removes willpower from the equation. It syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your actual work rhythm.

When the system is automatic, consistency becomes effortless.

Rick P. went from prediabetic A1C of 6.2 to optimal 5.4 in 6 months. Not because he found more willpower. Because he followed a system designed for his constraints.

Stop blaming yourself for lacking discipline. Start building systems that make discipline irrelevant.

---

## POST 4 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach test  

Your business dinner strategy is sabotaging your transformation.

Most executives think they have to choose between networking and nutrition.

Wrong.

Here's how to handle client dinners without derailing your progress:

**Before you go:**
- Eat a small protein snack 30 minutes before
- Review the menu online and pick your meal in advance

**At the restaurant:**
- Order first (don't get swayed by others' choices)
- Ask for dressing/sauce on the side
- Focus on protein + vegetables, limit the bread basket

**The conversation trick:**
Be the one asking questions. When you're talking less, you're eating slower and making better choices.

**Post-dinner:**
Don't try to "make up for it" the next day with extreme restriction. Just get back to your normal routine.

Business dinners are part of your life. The goal is to navigate them strategically, not avoid them completely.

What's your biggest challenge with staying on track during business meals?

---

## POST 5 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Engaged warm audience  

Dan M. was exactly where you are.

SaaS CEO. Burned out. 42 lbs over his target weight.

Hadn't trained consistently in 3 years because every program assumed he had a predictable schedule.

Local trainers kept getting canceled due to business emergencies. His Peloton collected dust.

Sound familiar?

Six months later:
→ Lost 42 lbs
→ Squats 2x his bodyweight  
→ Travels 2 weeks per month and stays perfectly on track

What changed? He stopped trying to force generic programs into his executive lifestyle.

Instead, he used The Performance Stack — a system designed specifically for executives who work 60-hour weeks.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

The difference wasn't more willpower. It was the right system for his actual constraints.

If you're tired of starting over every few months, maybe it's time to try an approach built for your real life.

---

## POST 6 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Fitness-interested executives  

"Can you train me over Zoom?"

That question changed everything.

It was March 2020. My gym was struggling. This client couldn't come in person due to COVID restrictions.

I almost said no. "Real training" happened in person, right?

But I needed the revenue, so I said yes.

That 60-minute Zoom session opened my eyes.

He got a better workout than most of my in-person clients. No commute time. No waiting for equipment. No gym politics.

Just focused, efficient training that fit his actual schedule.

I realized I'd been thinking about fitness all wrong.

The best system isn't the one that looks most "professional." It's the one that actually works with your real life.

That single session led me to completely rebuild my approach around what executives actually need:

Systems that travel. Accountability that works across time zones. Programs that adapt to unpredictable schedules.

Sometimes the biggest breakthroughs come from being forced to try something you never would have chosen.

---

## POST 7 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Warm audience, video viewers  

There are two types of executives:

Those who keep saying "I'll start Monday" and those who actually start.

If you're tired of catching your reflection in elevator mirrors and immediately looking away...

If you're exhausted by 3pm despite multiple cups of coffee...

If you're dreading your next physical because you know what the scale will say...

Then it's time to stop waiting for the perfect moment.

The Executive Athlete Program is for male executives age 40-55 who:
→ Run their own companies
→ Make $300K+ annually  
→ Haven't prioritized their health in years
→ Are done with generic programs that don't fit their real life

This isn't for everyone. It's specifically designed for executives who understand that their health is the foundation everything else is built on.

If that's you, the application takes 5 minutes and you'll hear back within 24 hours.

Apply at: reedperformance.co/apply

---

## POST 8 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach test  

Your energy crashes aren't random.

They're predictable. And preventable.

Most executives hit a wall around 3pm because they're running on caffeine and willpower instead of proper fuel.

Here's the energy management system that keeps you sharp all day:

**Morning (6-8am):**
Protein within 30 minutes of waking. Skip the pastry, grab eggs or Greek yogurt.

**Mid-morning (10am):**
Small protein snack if you have back-to-back meetings. Handful of nuts works.

**Lunch (12-1pm):**
Protein + vegetables + healthy fat. Avoid the carb crash from pasta or sandwiches.

**Afternoon (3pm):**
This is when most people reach for sugar. Instead: 10 push-ups or a 2-minute walk.

**Evening (6-8pm):**
Balanced meal, but don't eat 3 hours before bed. Your sleep quality affects tomorrow's energy.

The goal isn't perfection. It's consistency.

When your energy stays stable, your decision-making improves. Better decisions compound into better results.

What time of day do you typically crash? Let me know in the comments.

---

## POST 9 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Executives who've tried online programs  

"Online coaching can't replace having someone there in person."

I used to believe this too.

Then I realized something: the most effective business systems work across time zones and adapt to changing schedules.

Your fitness system should have the same flexibility.

Here's what actually matters for results:
→ Consistent execution of the right program
→ Real-time feedback and adjustments
→ Accountability that works with your schedule

Location is irrelevant.

Dan travels constantly for his SaaS company. He's trained in hotel rooms in Austin, New York, and Tokyo.

His results? 42 lbs lost, squats 2x bodyweight, hasn't missed a workout in 6 months.

The system travels with him. Video check-ins happen on his schedule. Slack support works across time zones.

He gets better accountability than he ever had with local trainers because the system is designed around his constraints, not a trainer's schedule.

The best accountability isn't someone standing over you. It's a system that makes consistency automatic regardless of location.

---

## POST 10 — IDENTITY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Business owners, high achievers  

There are two types of executives:

**The Peloton Paperweight CEO:**
→ Catches his reflection in elevator mirrors and immediately looks away
→ Exhausted by 3pm despite multiple cups of coffee
→ Dreading his next physical and the inevitable lecture
→ Spends $500/month on unused memberships and canceled trainers
→ Keeps saying "I'll start after this quarter ends"

**The Executive Athlete:**
→ Takes his body as seriously as his P&L
→ Energy stays consistent from 6am to 9pm without crashes
→ Confident in his physical presence during important meetings
→ Trains on his own terms with systems that sync to his 60-hour work week
→ Other CEOs ask what his secret is

You're either becoming one or staying the other.

The choice is yours.

But remember: every month of delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58.

Which identity are you choosing today?

---

## POST 11 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Health-conscious executives  

Rick P.'s doctor couldn't believe the numbers.

A1C dropped from 6.2 (prediabetic) to 5.4 (optimal).
28 lbs lost in 6 months.
Blood pressure normalized.

Rick is a private equity partner. Works 70-hour weeks. Travels constantly for deals.

The Performance Stack changed everything.

Custom nutrition protocols that work during client dinners. Training programs that adapt to hotel gyms. Accountability that respects his business priorities.

"My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

The transformation wasn't just physical. His energy improved. His decision-making got sharper. His confidence in meetings went up.

When executives optimize their health, everything else improves.

Your body is the platform everything else runs on. Invest in it accordingly.

---

## POST 12 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach test  

You're measuring the wrong metrics.

Most executives obsess over the scale. Wrong focus.

The scale doesn't tell you if you're building muscle, losing fat, or just dehydrated.

Here are the metrics that actually matter:

**Energy levels:** Rate your energy 1-10 each afternoon. Consistency matters more than peaks.

**Strength progression:** Can you do more push-ups this week than last week?

**Sleep quality:** Are you waking up rested or hitting snooze 3 times?

**Clothes fit:** How do your pants feel? This shows body composition changes the scale misses.

**Performance at work:** Are you sharper in afternoon meetings?

The scale is just one data point. Your transformation is much bigger than a number.

Track what actually impacts your life: energy, strength, confidence, performance.

When those improve, the scale follows.

What metric has been most motivating for you? Drop it in the comments.

---

## POST 13 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Engaged warm audience  

Every month you hesitate, you're another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58.

Plus $500/month wasted on unused Peloton memberships and canceled trainers.

The math is simple: inaction is more expensive than action.

The Executive Athlete Program is specifically designed for executives who are done making excuses and ready to make changes.

This isn't for everyone:

❌ Don't apply if you're looking for a magic pill
❌ Don't apply if you want someone to coddle you  
❌ Don't apply if you're not willing to follow a proven system

✅ DO apply if you're ready to take your body as seriously as your P&L
✅ DO apply if you want a system that works with your 60-hour weeks
✅ DO apply if you're tired of starting over every few months

The application takes 5 minutes. You'll hear back within 24 hours.

Either you're ready to become The Executive Athlete, or you're not.

Apply at: reedperformance.co/apply

---

## POST 14 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: High-income professionals  

You'll hire experts for everything except your health.

Think about it:

→ Legal issues? You hire the best attorney money can buy.
→ Tax problems? You get a top-tier CPA.
→ Business strategy? You bring in consultants.
→ Investment decisions? You work with financial advisors.

But when it comes to your health — the foundation everything else is built on — you try to figure it out yourself.

You download apps. You watch YouTube videos. You buy equipment and hope for the best.

Why?

Because you think fitness should be "simple" and you should be able to figure it out alone.

Here's the truth: transforming your body while maintaining a 60-hour work week is a complex problem that requires specialized expertise.

The same strategic thinking that makes you successful in business — leveraging expertise, following proven systems, avoiding unnecessary risks — applies to your health.

What takes 3+ years to figure out alone takes 6 months with expert guidance.

Your time is worth more than the learning curve.

---

## POST 15 — POLARIZING/FILTER POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Audience filtering  

Unpopular opinion: If you're not willing to invest in your health, you don't actually want to change.

You want to WANT to change. There's a difference.

I see executives all the time who:
→ Complain about being out of shape
→ Research programs for months
→ Ask for "free advice" 
→ Then do nothing

Listen, I get it. $12,000 is a significant investment.

But you spend more than that on:
→ Business consulting you don't implement
→ Marketing that doesn't work  
→ Equipment that sits unused
→ Memberships you never use

Your health is the foundation everything else is built on. If you won't invest in that, you're not serious about change.

If this offends you, that's fine. This isn't for you anyway.

The Executive Athlete Program is for executives who understand that premium results require premium investment.

## POST 16 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Business transformation stories  

I was the fat kid nobody believed in.

Coaches overlooked me. Classmates made jokes. Even I didn't believe I could be athletic.

That experience taught me something important: the biggest transformations happen when someone finally believes in themselves.

Now I help executives who've given up on themselves prove their own critics wrong.

The 45-year-old CEO who thinks he's "too old" to get in shape.

The executive who's convinced his schedule makes fitness impossible.

The business owner who believes he's tried everything and nothing works.

They're all wrong. But they don't know it yet.

My job isn't just to design workout programs. It's to help successful people see what's possible when they apply the same strategic thinking to their health that they apply to their business.

Every transformation starts with someone believing it's possible.

I was the fat kid nobody believed in. Now I help executives become the athletes nobody expected.

The best revenge is becoming who your critics said you could never be.

## POST 17 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach test  

Your workout is failing because it's too complicated.

Most executives try to do everything: cardio, weights, mobility, core work, stretching.

Result? They spend 90 minutes in the gym and feel overwhelmed.

Here's what actually works for busy schedules:

**Pick 3 movements:**
1. A squat variation (bodyweight, goblet, or barbell)
2. A push variation (push-ups, overhead press, or bench)
3. A pull variation (rows, pull-ups, or lat pulldowns)

**Do them 3 times per week:**
- 3 sets of each movement
- 8-12 reps per set
- 2-3 minutes rest between sets

Total time: 30 minutes including warm-up.

That's it.

Progressive overload beats program complexity every time.

Add weight, reps, or sets each week. Track your progress.

Consistency with simple beats perfection with complex.

Dan squats 2x his bodyweight using this approach. He's never spent more than 45 minutes training.

What's your current workout taking too long because it's too complicated?

---

## POST 18 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Warm audience, case study viewers  

The numbers don't lie:

52 out of 60 clients achieved their target results.

That's an 87% success rate.

Clients drop 5-8 lbs and feel noticeably stronger before month 1 ends.

The Performance Stack removes guesswork. Custom baselines. Proven protocols. Real-time adjustments.

The 8 clients who didn't hit their targets? They stopped following the system.

Success isn't about perfection. It's about consistency.

The system works. The question is: will you work the system?

If you're ready to become client #53, the application takes 5 minutes.

reedperformance.co/apply

---

## POST 19 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Time-constrained executives  

"I don't have time to focus on fitness."

I hear this from executives making $500K+ who spend 2 hours a day on email.

You have time. You're just not prioritizing correctly.

Here's the truth: your health is your #1 priority because it's the foundation that makes everything else possible.

Every hour you invest in fitness returns 3-4 hours of higher-quality performance.

Better energy = better decisions.
Better decisions = better results.
Better results = better business outcomes.

You wouldn't skip oil changes on a Ferrari because you're "too busy driving."

The busier your schedule, the more critical it becomes to maintain the engine that powers everything else.

Your body is that engine.

4 hours per week in The Performance Stack gives you back 15+ hours of peak performance.

You can't afford NOT to prioritize this.

---

## POST 20 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target: Highly engaged audience  

Two roads:

**Road 1:** Keep doing what you're doing. Catch your reflection in elevator mirrors and look away. Energy crashes by 3pm. Dread your next physical. Watch younger executives outlift you at company retreats.

**Road 2:** Become The Executive Athlete. Take your body as seriously as your P&L. Energy stays consistent through 12-hour days. Confident in every boardroom. Other CEOs ask what your secret is.

Same amount of time passes either way.

The only difference is the choice you make today.

The Executive Athlete Program starts with a simple application. 5 minutes of your time for a chance to transform the next 6 months.

But here's the thing: I only work with 12 clients at a time.

If you're ready to stop making excuses and start making changes, apply now.

If you're going to "think about it" for another few months, save us both some time and don't apply.

The choice is yours.

reedperformance.co/apply

# Facebook/Instagram Ad Variations for Reed Performance

---

## Ad 1: Problem Agitation (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Another Peloton collecting dust? You're not broken. The system is.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Another Peloton collecting dust? You're not broken. The system is.

Here's the thing most executives don't realize: every fitness program you've tried was designed for people who DON'T run companies.

Generic workout plans. Trainers who don't understand business travel. Apps that assume you have 90 minutes a day to meal prep.

No wonder you've started and stopped 3+ times.

I spent 11 years training executives who make $300K+ and run their own companies. Here's what I learned: you don't need more willpower. You need a system that fits the reality of a 60-hour work week.

The Performance Stack syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your actual schedule — not some fantasy where you have unlimited time.

Dan M. (SaaS CEO) was 42 lbs over target and hadn't trained in 3 years. Six months later? Lost 42 lbs, squats 2x bodyweight, travels 2 weeks/month and stays on track.

"Marcus is the only trainer who actually gets what my life is like."

If you're tired of programs that ignore your reality, let's talk.

**Headline:** Stop Fighting Your Schedule

**Description:** Built for 60-hour work weeks

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Visual:** Split image: dusty Peloton on left, executive working out in home office on right

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (interests: Peloton, executive coaching, business ownership)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Budget: $50-100/day

---

## Ad 2: Social Proof Story (Warm Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Rick P. went from prediabetic to shocking his doctor in 6 months.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Rick P. went from prediabetic to shocking his doctor in 6 months.

Private equity partner. A1C of 6.2. Doctor said he was headed for diabetes.

Rick had tried everything: personal trainers he canceled on, MyFitnessPal he abandoned after 2 weeks, that Peloton in his basement collecting dust.

The problem wasn't willpower. It was that every approach ignored his reality: 60-hour weeks, constant business travel, stress eating between meetings.

Six months in The Executive Athlete Program: A1C dropped to 5.4, lost 28 lbs.

"My doctor couldn't believe the numbers."

Here's what worked: The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to the rhythm of a 60-hour work week.

Custom home programs. Nutrition that works during business travel. Training through jet lag. Hotel gym protocols.

No generic bullshit. No cookie-cutter plans.

If you're an executive who's tried 3+ approaches without lasting results, this is built for you.

**Headline:** From Prediabetic to Peak Performance

**Description:** Real executive transformation

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

- Budget: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]-150/day

## Ad 3: Lead Magnet (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
The 15-minute workout that fits between meetings.

**Primary Text (Long):**
The 15-minute workout that fits between meetings.

You guys know the drill. Another day, another excuse: "I'll start after this quarter ends."

Meanwhile, you're catching your reflection in the elevator mirror each morning and not liking what you see.

Listen, I've trained 340 executives over 11 years. The ones who succeed don't have more time than you. They have a system that works with their schedule, not against it.

That's why I created The Executive's 15-Minute Workout.

No gym required. No equipment needed. Just you, your home office, and 15 minutes between calls.

This isn't some watered-down routine. It's the exact protocol my $300K+ clients use when they're slammed with back-to-back meetings.

Download it free. Try it for a week. See what happens when fitness finally fits your reality.

Because here's the thing: every month you delay is another month closer to the heart attack your father had at 58.

Stop leaving your health to chance.

**Headline:** 15 Minutes Between Meetings

**Description:** No gym. No equipment.

**CTA Button:** Download

**Suggested Visual:** Executive in business attire doing bodyweight exercise in office setting

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (interests: executive fitness, home workouts, busy professionals)
- Optimization: Lead generation
- Budget: $30-75/day

---

## Ad 4: Retargeting / Belief-Shift Content (No Hard CTA)

**Primary Text (Short):**
"I don't have time" is the excuse. Here's the real problem.

**Primary Text (Long):**
"I don't have time" is the excuse. Here's the real problem.

You guys run companies that generate $500K to $5M in revenue. You manage teams, close deals, make decisions that affect dozens of employees.

But when it comes to your health? "I don't have time."

Here's what I learned training executives for 11 years: time isn't your problem. Priority is.

You FIND time for what matters. Emergency client calls. Board meetings. Your kid's school play.

The real issue? You've been sold fitness programs designed for people who DON'T run companies.

90-minute gym sessions. Meal prep on Sundays. Training schedules that assume you're home by 6pm every night.

No wonder you think you don't have time.

But what if fitness worked WITH your schedule instead of against it?

What if you could train in your home office between calls? Eat right during business travel? Stay consistent through your busiest quarters?

## Ad 5: Direct Offer (Warm Audience Only)

Ready to become the executive athlete? Here's how.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Ready to become the executive athlete? Here's how.

You know who you want to be: a high performer who takes his body as seriously as his P&L.

Confident. Capable. Energetic. Proud of how you look.

The guy who takes off his shirt at the beach without sucking in. Who doesn't avoid photos at company events. Who has energy left for his kids after a 12-hour day.

The Executive Athlete Program makes this your reality.

6 months. 3 phases:
• Assessment: Custom baseline + bloodwork review
• Rebuild: 8 weeks in-home strength + nutrition protocol (first 15-20 lb loss)
• Scale: 12 weeks progressive loading + travel protocols (transformation + maintenance)

Custom training program. Nutrition that works during business travel. Weekly video check-ins. Private Slack access. Hotel gym protocols.

The Performance Stack — a 3-layer system that syncs training, nutrition, and recovery to your 60-hour work week.

$12,000 for 6 months (or 6 monthly payments of $2,100).

This is for executives who make $300K+, run their own companies, and are done making excuses.

If that's you, let's talk.

**CTA Button:** Apply Now
