If you're ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man, take my free Second-Act Dating Audit.

"The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program." — Dr. James K., cardiac surgeon

**Primary Text (Long):**
"The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program." — Dr. James K., cardiac surgeon

Dr. James had been divorced 3 years. Zero second dates in 18 months. Making great money, successful career, but completely invisible to the women he actually wanted to meet.

Sound familiar?

After 6 months in The Second Act Method, James got engaged to a woman he met at our November retreat.

What changed? He stopped dating from the wound of his divorce and started dating from wholeness.

Here's what James learned (and what every divorced man over 45 needs to understand):

Dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

You're unconsciously seeking women who remind you of what you're trying to escape. You're dating to prove your ex wrong, not to find your right person.

The Second Act Method is a 6-month Gottman-based protocol that rebuilds your attachment capacity after divorce.

Phase 1: Grounding (4 weeks of post-divorce identity work)
Phase 2: Rebuilding (8 weeks of attachment pattern work)

Result? You become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man — a guy who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is.

In 9 years, I've helped 420 men find real partners. Not by getting more matches. By healing their dating frame.

Ready to stop dating from your wound?

**Headline:** Surgeon: "Only Reason I'm Not Alone"

**Description:** How he found love after 3 years single

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

---

## Ad 4: Belief-Shift Content (Retargeting - No CTA)

**Primary Text (Short):**
You think you need better matches. You actually need a better dating frame.

**Primary Text (Long):**
You think you need better matches. You actually need a better dating frame.

Here's what I mean...

Most divorced men approach dating like they're shopping for a car. They make a list of features they want, swipe through the inventory, and get frustrated when nothing "fits."

But dating isn't shopping. It's magnetism.

When you date from the wound of your divorce, you unconsciously attract women who remind you of what you're trying to escape. Your energy is saying "prove I'm worthy" instead of "let's see if we're a match."

That's why the same type of woman keeps showing up. That's why every relationship feels like déjà vu.

The apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

I spent 18 months after my divorce dating badly. Angry, distrustful, attracting the wrong women. I thought I needed better matches.

My mentor told me: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

He was right.

The moment I shifted from dating to escape my past to dating from my authentic self, everything changed. Different women started showing up. Better conversations. Real connections.

That's the difference between dating from the wound and dating from wholeness.

One repels your right person. The other attracts them.

Which frame are you dating from?

- Daily budget: $30-50

---

If you're ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man, here's how...

**Primary Text (Long):**

relationship-dense.content:
# YouTube Video Script: "The Real Reason You Keep Attracting Women Who Remind You of Your Ex"

**Video Title:** "The Real Reason You Keep Attracting Women Who Remind You of Your Ex (It's Not What You Think)"

**Thumbnail Text:** "SAME WOMAN, DIFFERENT FACE"

**Core Belief Shift:** From "I just need better matches and dating apps are broken" → "I'm dating from the wound of my divorce instead of from emotional wholeness"

**Target Audience:** Divorced men 45-60 making $200K+ who've been single 1-4 years with no dating traction

**Estimated Runtime:** 12-15 minutes (~2,100 words)

---

## HOOK (0:00-0:30) [~75 words]

Listen, if you're a successful divorced man over 45 who keeps matching with women who feel eerily familiar — the same drama, the same emotional unavailability, the same patterns that broke your first marriage — this video is going to break that cycle for good.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Why You Attract the Same Type Every Time"]

I'm going to show you how to stop attracting women who remind you of your ex without avoiding vulnerability, so you can choose from wholeness and feel at peace with your history. And if you're tired of first dates that feel like déjà vu, pay attention to what I'm about to share.

---

## INTRO (0:30-1:30) [~150 words]

My name is David Alvarez. I help divorced men over 45 find loving relationships without repeating the patterns that broke the first one. In the last 9 years, I've coached 420 men, and I've seen this pattern destroy more second chances at love than anything else.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "David Alvarez - 420 Men Coached - MA Psychology NYU"]

Here's what you're going to learn in this video: First, the real reason you keep attracting the same type of woman — and it's not what you think. Second, the psychological pattern that's sabotaging your partner selection without you realizing it. And third, the exact process I use with clients to break this cycle and start attracting emotionally available women.

Now, this video is for divorced men who make $200K+ and have been single for 1-4 years with no real dating progress. If you're looking for pickup tactics or ways to date women half your age, this isn't for you. But if you're ready to understand why your dating life feels like Groundhog Day, keep watching.

---

## PROBLEM (1:30-4:00) [~375 words]

Here's what most divorced men believe: "The problem is these dating apps are broken. They're not showing my profile to quality women. If I could just get better matches, I'd find someone different."

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "The Broken App Theory"]

Sound familiar? You probably think if you could just optimize your photos, write better messages, or try a different app, you'd finally meet someone who's nothing like your ex-wife.

But here's the brutal truth — and I know this because I lived it myself for 18 months after my divorce. You're not attracting the same type of woman because of bad luck or broken algorithms. You're attracting them because you're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from emotional wholeness.

[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story]
After my own divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I was angry, distrustful, and kept attracting women who reminded me of my ex. I was 38 years old, making $180K in a private therapy practice, and still sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental. I thought the problem was that Charlotte didn't have enough quality women. I thought the problem was that dating apps were superficial. I thought the problem was everything except me.

Every first date felt like I was interviewing a potential replacement for what I'd lost. Every conversation was me unconsciously trying to prove my ex wrong instead of proving myself right. And guess what happened? I kept attracting women who were just as wounded, just as defensive, just as emotionally unavailable as I was.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Dating from Wounds = Attracting Wounded People"]

Here's what this looks like in real life: You match with a woman who seems perfect on paper. Successful, attractive, says she wants a relationship. But then she starts pulling away after a few dates. She's hot and cold with her communication. She talks about her ex constantly. She's suspicious of your motives. She creates drama where none existed.

Sound familiar? That's because she's mirroring your own emotional state back to you. You're both dating from wounds instead of wholeness, and wounded people attract wounded people like magnets.

The consequence of believing "I just need better matches"? You waste years swiping through thousands of profiles, burning $400-600 monthly on premium subscriptions, going on first dates that feel like job interviews, and wondering why every woman you meet has "so much baggage."

---

## NEW BELIEF / PARADIGM SHIFT (4:00-8:00) [~600 words]

Here's the truth that changed everything for me and my clients: You don't need better matches. You need better emotional patterns. You're dating from the wound of your divorce, and that wound determines who you attract and how every interaction unfolds.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "You Attract Who You Are, Not Who You Want"]

[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy]
Think of it like this — you wouldn't try to fix a car's engine problems by going to better gas stations. If your engine has issues, it doesn't matter if you use premium fuel from the fanciest station in town — you'll still break down on the highway. Dating apps are just the gas station. Your emotional baseline and attachment patterns are the engine that determines how every relationship runs.

[PROOF BLOCK: Authority Reference]
In "Attached" by Amir Levine and Rachel Heller, the research shows that our attachment style — formed in childhood and reinforced through major relationships like marriage — determines who we're drawn to and how we behave in relationships. Divorce often activates anxious or avoidant attachment patterns that must be healed before healthy relationships become possible.

[PROOF BLOCK: Logic Trap]
Here's the logic trap that makes this undeniable: If better matches were really the solution, then every successful, attractive divorced man would find love quickly on dating apps. But that's not what happens, is it? Some of the most successful men I know — surgeons, executives, business owners — spend years striking out on apps while guys with less going for them find lasting relationships. The difference isn't their market value; it's their emotional availability.

[PROOF BLOCK: Client Story]
Let me tell you about Dr. James K., a cardiac surgeon who came to me after being divorced 3 years with zero second dates in 18 months. This guy had everything going for him — great career, financial stability, attractive, intelligent. But he was dating from the wound of his divorce, which made him come across as guarded and judgmental on every first date.

Women could sense his emotional unavailability even though he desperately wanted a relationship. He was unconsciously screening for women who wouldn't trigger his fear of being hurt again, which meant he was only attracted to emotionally distant women. Through our 6-month program, he rebuilt his attachment capacity and learned to date from wholeness instead of wounds. Result? He got engaged to a woman he met at one of our retreats in 8 months.

[PROOF BLOCK: Cross-Domain Reference]
You already know this works in business. When your company had problems, you didn't blame the market or the customers — you looked at your systems, your processes, your leadership. You took ownership and fixed what was broken internally. That's why you're successful now. Apply that same ownership mentality to your dating life.

[PROOF BLOCK: Reframe]
Here's the reframe that changes everything: What if your divorce wasn't the end of your love story? What if it was the beginning of your emotional education? What if every woman who reminds you of your ex is actually showing you exactly what needs to be healed before you can attract someone different?

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Divorce = Emotional Education"]

The moment I understood this — that I was dating from wounds instead of wholeness — everything changed. I stopped trying to prove my ex wrong and started proving myself right. I stopped looking for someone to fix my loneliness and started healing my capacity for genuine partnership.

[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story Continuation]
My mentor told me something that broke me open: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails." That's when I realized I needed to do the emotional work first. I spent 2 years in my own therapy, closed the private practice, and launched a dating program specifically for men coming out of divorce. Today I have a 3000-person community and I'm remarried to a woman I met through my own program.

The new belief is this: The Second Act Method — a 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce — is the proven path to attracting emotionally available partners.

---

## PROOF / STORIES (8:00-12:00) [~600 words]

Let me show you how this plays out in real life with three different men who made this shift.

**Carlos M.'s Story:**
Carlos was a restaurant owner, divorced 2 years, and he was drinking too much while dating women 15 years younger. He kept complaining that women his age had "too much baggage" and were "too serious." But here's what was really happening — he was avoiding age-appropriate women because they triggered his fear of another serious relationship.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Avoidance = Unhealed Attachment Wound"]

Carlos was dating from the wound of his divorce. He was terrified of getting hurt again, so he unconsciously chose women who couldn't hurt him because they weren't emotionally available for anything real. The 25-year-olds he dated were fun distractions, but they kept him stuck in a pattern of avoiding genuine intimacy.

Through our program, Carlos realized he wasn't just avoiding the wrong women — he was avoiding himself. He got sober, did the attachment work, and started dating from wholeness instead of wounds. Result? He's been in a healthy relationship with an age-appropriate partner for 9 months now. As he told me: "I stopped running from myself."

**Mike D.'s Journey:**
Mike was a tech founder who took the opposite approach — he avoided dating entirely for 18 months after his divorce. He thought he needed more general healing before he'd be "ready" for a relationship. He was in individual therapy, reading self-help books, waiting for the perfect moment when he felt completely healed.

But here's what Mike discovered: you can't heal attachment wounds in isolation. You have to practice new patterns in relationship contexts. Mike was stuck in analysis mode, intellectually understanding his patterns without emotionally transforming them.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "You Can't Heal Attachment Wounds in Isolation"]

When Mike joined our program, he realized that dating-specific work was different from general therapy. He needed to practice vulnerability with other men who understood his struggle, learn to spot emotional availability in potential partners, and rebuild his capacity for trust in romantic contexts. 

Within 11 months, Mike was in a committed relationship with someone he calls "the right person at the right time." His breakthrough moment came when he realized: "I wasn't broken. I was healing."

**My Own Transformation:**
Here's how this shift changed my life. Before I understood I was dating from wounds, every interaction with women was filtered through my divorce trauma. I was hypervigilant for signs of betrayal. I was defensive about my story. I was performing instead of being authentic.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Performance vs. Authenticity"]

I remember one date where a woman asked about my divorce, and I launched into this defensive explanation about how my ex was the problem and I was the victim. The woman's energy completely shifted. She could sense I was still emotionally married to my ex-wife, just in a negative way.

After I did the attachment work and learned to date from wholeness, everything changed. I could talk about my divorce as a growth experience instead of a trauma story. I could be curious about potential partners instead of suspicious. I could be vulnerable without being desperate.

That's how I met my current wife in 2021 — not by finding better matches, but by becoming a better match. She was attracted to my emotional intelligence and the way I'd transformed my divorce pain into relationship wisdom.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Pain → Wisdom = Attraction"]

The pattern is always the same: men who do the inner work attract women who've done their inner work. Men who date from wounds attract women who are dating from wounds. You get what you are, not what you want.

---

## ACTION STEPS (12:00-15:00) [~450 words]

Alright, so how do you start dating from wholeness instead of wounds? Here are four concrete steps you can take today:

**Step 1: Audit Your Attraction Patterns**
[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Step 1: Audit Your Patterns"]

Look at the last five women you were seriously interested in. Write down their emotional patterns — were they available or unavailable? Secure or anxious? Healed or wounded? If you see a pattern of being drawn to emotionally unavailable women, that's your wound talking, not your wisdom.

**Step 2: Identify Your Divorce Triggers**
What specific behaviors or situations in dating trigger your divorce trauma? Is it when women don't respond quickly to texts? When they mention their ex? When they seem too independent? These triggers reveal exactly what needs to be healed before you can choose partners consciously instead of reactively.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Triggers = Healing Opportunities"]

**Step 3: Practice Emotional Regulation**
Before your next date, spend 10 minutes getting grounded. Ask yourself: "Am I going into this interaction to prove something, or to discover something?" If you're trying to prove your worth or prove your ex wrong, you're dating from wounds. If you're curious about genuine compatibility, you're dating from wholeness.

**Step 4: Get Support from Other Men**
You can't heal attachment wounds in isolation. Find other divorced men who are committed to doing this work. Share your patterns, get feedback, practice vulnerability in a safe context. This is why our Brotherhood community has 420 success stories — men heal faster in community than they do alone.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Community Accelerates Healing"]

Here's what to expect when you implement these steps: Within 30 days, you'll notice a shift in how you show up on dates. You'll be more present, less defensive, more curious about who she is instead of worried about what she thinks of you. Women will respond differently because you're operating from a different emotional frequency.

The women who were attracted to your wounded energy will lose interest, and that's a good thing. The women who are attracted to your healed energy will show up differently — more available, more authentic, more interested in real connection.

Remember, this isn't about becoming perfect before you date. It's about becoming conscious of your patterns so you can choose from awareness instead of reacting from wounds.

---

## CTA (15:00-End) [~300 words]

So remember — it's not about finding better matches on dating apps. It's about healing your attachment patterns so you become the kind of man that emotionally healthy women choose.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Heal Your Patterns = Attract Healthy Partners"]

Imagine waking up next to someone who chose you, not settled for you. Someone who sees your divorce story as wisdom, not baggage. Someone who's attracted to your emotional intelligence and the way you've transformed your pain into growth. Your kids see you in a healthy relationship. You're at peace with your history and excited about your future.

That's what becomes possible when you stop dating from wounds and start dating from wholeness.

If you want to discover which specific attachment wounds are sabotaging your dating success, I've created a free assessment called The Second-Act Dating Audit. It takes 5 minutes and gives you a personalized report on your attachment patterns and what to heal first.

[ON-SCREEN TEXT: "Free Assessment: SecondActDating.com/audit"]

You can find the link in the description below.

And if you're ready to go deeper and want to work with me personally to break these patterns for good, The Second Act Method is my 6-month program that helps divorced men find lasting love using Gottman-based attachment healing. We have a 12-month guarantee — if you don't have three meaningful dates per month by month 4, we extend your program for free.

If this video helped you see your dating patterns in a new light, hit the subscribe button and the bell — I post new videos every week on post-divorce dating success.

# Instagram/Facebook Carousel Collection
## Second Act Dating — Post-Divorce Dating Success

---

## Carousel #1 — Educational
**Carousel Title:** The 3-Phase Second Act Method Breakdown
**Core Belief Targeted:** #1 (Core Belief — The Second Act Method is the proven path)
**Funnel Stage:** MOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Stop Swiping. Start Healing.
**Body Text:** The Second Act Method: 3 phases that transform wounded swipers into men who get chosen.
**Visual Direction:** Dark navy background, bold white text, minimal design with arrow pointing right

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** Phase 1: Grounding
**Body Text:** 4 weeks of post-divorce identity work. Emotional baseline reset. No more dating from the wound.
**Visual Direction:** Clean white background, navy text, simple foundation icon

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Phase 2: Rebuilding
**Body Text:** 8 weeks of attachment pattern work. New dating frame established using Gottman Method principles.
**Visual Direction:** Light gray background, navy text, building blocks or construction icon

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** Phase 3: Attracting
**Body Text:** 12 weeks of real-world dating practice. Consistent dates with right-fit women who see your growth.
**Visual Direction:** Navy background, white text, magnetic or attraction icon

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** The Result
**Body Text:** From invisible and angry to selective and confident. 63 out of 80 men found lasting love.
**Visual Direction:** Gold/yellow background, dark text, checkmark or trophy icon

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Ready to Start?
**Body Text:** Comment METHOD below and I'll send you the complete phase breakdown.
**Visual Direction:** Call-to-action slide with contrasting colors, arrow pointing to comment section

**Caption:**
Most divorced men think they need better matches. They actually need better emotional patterns.

After my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly — angry, distrustful, attracting women who reminded me of my ex. I was making $180K but sleeping on a mattress on the floor.

The breakthrough came when I realized I wasn't dating to find a partner. I was dating to prove my ex wrong.

That's when I developed The Second Act Method. Three phases that address the root cause: your attachment patterns, not your dating pool.

Phase 1 gives you emotional stability. Phase 2 rebuilds healthy relationship patterns. Phase 3 puts you in front of women who appreciate emotional intelligence.

The result? You stop getting ghosted and start getting chosen.

**ManyChat Trigger:** Comment "METHOD" → Receive "The Complete Second Act Method Phase Breakdown PDF"

---

## Carousel #2 — Belief-Shifting
**Carousel Title:** Old Way vs Your Way — Dating After Divorce
**Core Belief Targeted:** #2 (Real Problem — Dating from wound vs wholeness)
**Funnel Stage:** MOF

### Slide 1
**Headline Text:** Stop Dating From Your Wound
**Body Text:** Here's what actually works for divorced men over 45.
**Visual Direction:** Bold red "STOP" sign background, white text

### Slide 2
**Headline Text:** The Old Way
**Body Text:** Swipe endlessly hoping better matches fix your patterns. Send 30 messages weekly. Burn $600 monthly on apps.
**Visual Direction:** Faded red background, frustrated emoji, phone with dating app interface

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** Why It Fails
**Body Text:** You're dating from the wound of divorce. Attracting women who trigger the same patterns that broke your marriage.
**Visual Direction:** Dark background with broken heart or warning symbol

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** The New Way
**Body Text:** Heal attachment patterns first. Date from wholeness, not wounds. Attract emotionally available women.
**Visual Direction:** Clean green background, upward arrow, healing/growth imagery

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** Why It Works
**Body Text:** Dr. James K.: zero second dates for 18 months → engaged in 8 months. "The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program."
**Visual Direction:** Success green background, testimonial format with quote marks

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Your Choice
**Body Text:** Comment WHOLENESS and I'll send you the attachment pattern assessment.
**Visual Direction:** Split screen showing old way vs new way, arrow pointing to comment

**Caption:**
The brutal truth about dating after 45: You're not failing because the apps are broken.

You're failing because you're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from emotional wholeness.

Every swipe, every message, every first date is you unconsciously trying to prove your ex wrong instead of proving yourself right.

That's why you keep attracting women who remind you of exactly what you're trying to escape.

But when you heal those attachment patterns first — when you date from wholeness instead of wounds — everything changes.

You stop getting ghosted and start getting chosen. You stop settling and start selecting.

Carlos M. went from drinking and dating women 15 years younger to sober and committed with an age-appropriate partner. "I stopped running from myself."

The difference? He addressed the wound instead of avoiding it.

**ManyChat Trigger:** Comment "WHOLENESS" → Receive "The Attachment Pattern Assessment Quiz"

---

## Carousel #3 — Story
**Carousel Title:** From $180K and Sleeping on a Mattress to Remarried

**Funnel Stage:** MOF

**Headline Text:** The Struggle

**Visual Direction:** Gray stormy background, rain or storm imagery

**Headline Text:** The Turning Point

### Slide 5

**Visual Direction:** Success imagery, wedding rings, community symbols

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** Your Turn
**Body Text:** Comment STORY and I'll share the exact healing framework I used.
**Visual Direction:** Inspirational background, forward-pointing arrow

**Caption:**
After my divorce, I thought I just needed better women.

I was making $180K as a therapist but sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental. Every date was a disaster. I was angry, suspicious, and kept attracting women who triggered all my divorce wounds.

The breakthrough came when my mentor said: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

That broke me open.

I realized I was dating from the wound, not from wholeness. So I stopped dating entirely for 2 years. Did my own therapy. Closed the private practice. Built what became The Second Act Method.

Today I'm remarried to an incredible woman I met through the community. We have a life I never thought possible after divorce.

Your pain can become your purpose. Your wound can become your wisdom.

But first, you have to stop dating from it.

**ManyChat Trigger:** Comment "STORY" → Receive "The Wound-to-Wholeness Framework PDF"

---

"I was performing instead of being authentic," he told me later.

Through The Second Act Method, James learned to date from wholeness instead of wounds. He rebuilt his attachment capacity using Gottman Method principles.

The transformation was immediate. Better first dates. Real connections. Women who appreciated his emotional intelligence.

8 months later, he was engaged to a woman he met at our retreat.

Same as you.

Most divorced men don't realize they're dating from their wound until they see these 5 signs.

- I stopped attracting my ex in different bodies

- Women started choosing me, not ghosting me

Your dating results are a direct reflection of your emotional state.

Heal the wound, transform the results.

# Short-Form Video Scripts for David Alvarez

---

## Short #1 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Pattern Interrupt Hook
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok
**Duration:** 45 seconds

### Hook (On-Screen Text)
"STOP BLAMING THE DATING APPS"

### Spoken Script
Stop blaming the dating apps. Here's the brutal truth no one's telling you.

You think the problem is broken algorithms or low-quality matches, right? After coaching 420 divorced men, I can tell you that's bullshit.

The real problem? You're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from emotional wholeness. You're unconsciously attracting women who mirror your own emotional unavailability back to you.

I spent 18 months after my divorce thinking Charlotte didn't have quality women. Turns out, I was the problem. I was angry, distrustful, and every woman I met reminded me of my ex.

Your dating apps aren't broken. Your emotional patterns are. And wounded people attract wounded people like magnets.

The moment you heal your attachment patterns, everything changes. Better matches start showing up because you're finally showing up as a better match.

### On-Screen Text Overlays
- "STOP BLAMING THE DATING APPS"
- "420 men coached"
- "Dating from wounds = attracting wounded people"
- "Heal your patterns = better matches"

### Visual Direction
Talking head with confident, direct energy. Cut between close-up shots. Use text overlays for key statistics.

### CTA/Closing
"Comment 'PATTERNS' if you want to know which attachment wound is sabotaging your dating success."

### Hashtags
#DivorcedDad #DatingOver45 #AttachmentTheory #MensDating #SecondChances

---

## Short #2 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Old Belief vs New Belief
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, TikTok
**Duration:** 50 seconds

### Hook (On-Screen Text)
"WHY YOU ATTRACT THE SAME TYPE EVERY TIME"

### Spoken Script
Here's why you keep attracting women who remind you of your ex, and it's not what you think.

You believe: "I just need better matches. These apps are showing me the wrong women."

But here's the truth: You attract who you are, not who you want.

Think of it like trying to fix a car's engine problems by going to better gas stations. Doesn't matter if you use premium fuel from the fanciest station in town — you'll still break down if your engine has issues.

Dating apps are just the gas station. Your emotional baseline and attachment patterns are the engine that determines how every relationship runs.

I learned this the hard way. After my divorce, I kept matching with emotionally unavailable women because I was emotionally unavailable. I was dating from wounds instead of wholeness.

The moment I healed my attachment capacity, I started attracting emotionally healthy women. Now I'm remarried to someone I met through my own program.

Your secret fear is that you'll die alone in your apartment. And guess what? Women can sense that desperation from a mile away.

Your confidence in business needs to match your confidence in dating.
